name: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
meets_full_need_notes: Virginia Tech does not guarantee to meet full demonstrated financial need. Average need met is approximately 65-70%. Virginia Tech has one of the lowest average net prices among top public universities for low-income students.
no_loan_notes: Virginia Tech includes loans in most financial aid packages. No institutional no-loan policy. However, Virginia Tech has historically kept costs low and is known for strong value.

merit_thresholds.notes: Virginia Tech offers significant merit scholarships including the President's Scholarship, Corps of Cadets scholarships, and departmental awards. Many out-of-state merit scholarships significantly reduce the higher OOS tuition rate. National Merit finalists receive full tuition scholarships.

ed_aid_implications: Virginia Tech does not offer ED. EA applicants receive the same aid consideration as regular decision applicants.
special_programs: Hokie Scholarship (institutional aid for VA residents)
appeal_policy: Students may appeal financial aid decisions through the University Scholarships Office. Appeals should include documentation of changed family circumstances, medical expenses, or other unusual financial situations.
